title: Warden of Routine — Aftermath
description: The Warden of Routine sits on the curb and sets down her stop sign. She has never lost a crossing before. The neighborhood has never existed without her at this intersection. She realizes, for the first time, that she cannot remember being assigned here.

lines: The Warden set her stop sign carefully against the lamppost and sat on the curb.
The intersection was empty. It had been empty throughout. The signal said WALK, and the walk sign blinked once and went dark.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: I've never lost a crossing before.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: Thank you. For letting us through.

steps.text: A long pause. Something shifted in her posture — not defeat exactly, but a kind of attention turning inward.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: I've been here such a long time.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: I've been at this crosswalk for… how long?

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: It feels like longer than the neighborhood's been here.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: How long has the neighborhood been here?

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: Since the beginning. That's what I was told. This neighborhood has always been here. I have always been at this intersection.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: Except I can't remember the first day. I can't remember being assigned here. I just… was. And then I was here.

steps.text: She picked up the stop sign again, the automatic motion of someone who needs to be doing their job or the ground feels uncertain.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: You should cross now. Before traffic comes.

steps.text: There was, as there had been throughout, no traffic.
